articles.title: Woman's Sphere
articles.author: Ramona W. Cannon
articles.text:
JANE DELANO, who was honored during the March Red Cross drive, is sometimes called the Florence Nightingale of the Red Cross. She headed the first Red Cross Nurses organization and built up a large reserve group, carefully checking each woman's qualifications. The Government gratefully used these nurses during World War I, when Miss Delano herself went overseas, working, organizing, and strengthening the organization. She also organized the Red Cross Public Health Nursing Program, later taken over and still operated by the Government.

Her charity cases far exceeded her paid practice. At seventy-two she was especially honored at Atlantic City by the American Medical Association. In 1947 she was voted "Indian of the Year" by the intertribal council.

MARIA PIA, twenty, daughter of exiled King Umberto of Italy, was recently married in Portugal to Prince Alexander, thirty, son of Yugoslavia's former Prince Regent Paul.

IN Texas, a Constitutional Amendment, in November, gave women for the first time the right and responsibility to serve on juries, so that they are no longer "half-citizens." An attractive, intelligent-looking, all-woman jury was immediately chosen.

SENATOR MARGARET CHASE SMITH and Representative Frances Bolton have introduced resolutions in Congress asking that the rose be made America's national flower.

DR. LILLIE ROSE MINOKA-HILL (1876-1952) was honored last Memorial Day by the dedication of a granite monument near her former home in Oneida, Wisconsin. Both Indians and whites, including Catholics, Episcopalians, and Methodists all participated, representative of those for whom she had cared. A Mohawk orphan, reared by Quakers, she became a physician. When she was widowed and left to support five children, she returned to medical practice.

BIRTHDAY congratulations are extended to Mrs. Dessie Newman Middleton, ninety-seven, Los Angeles, California; Mrs. Ursula Bandley Gee, Provo, Utah, ninety-seven; Mrs. Janet Wade, Glendale, California, ninety-six; Mrs. Josephine Hansen, Logan, Utah, and Mrs. Amy Kuester, Grand Island, Nebraska, ninety-three; Mrs. Margaret King Walpole, Salt Lake City, Utah, ninety-one; Mrs. Elizabeth Ann Moffat Froerer, Ogden, Utah, ninety-one; and Mrs. Anna Jargena Christina Madsen Heder, ninety-one, Mesa, Arizona.
